← the Atlas

Features

index·evergreen·JUN 19, 2026

Proposed capabilities — designs for things kolu doesn't do yet, with the reasoning and the plan to build them.

The Features index gathers notes that propose a capability kolu doesn’t have yet: the design, the motivation, and enough of a plan to build it. A contributor proposal lands here too, carrying status: proposed until a maintainer flips it.

This note is an index (moc: true): a note is filed here by listing feature in its parents. The graph renders it large; the notes wired to it are its cluster, and its card on the Atlas lists them all.

Referenced by

  • @kolu/surface-daemon — one spine for kaval and odu serve
  • @kolu/surface-mcp — every surface an MCP server, honestly scoped
  • A CI runner you attach to — graduating mini-ci beyond justci
  • Canvas tiles that show their state — border prototypes
  • ChromeBar declutter — show the commit once
  • ChromeBar identity rail (srv · client · kaval)
  • Cite → open in the Code browser
  • Code browser preview → @kolu/solid-fileview
  • Distinguish the Active Terminal in a Split
  • Dock — telling repos apart at a glance
  • Git-status indicators in the Code-tab "All files" view
  • Graph view + Maps of Content
  • Kaval sessions — dial daemons, multiplex the canvas
  • kaval-tui — a CLI terminal client for kolu-server
  • Markdown preview: footnotes open in a click popover
  • Markdown preview: Obsidian-style [[wikilinks]]
  • Mobile key bar — two rows, no horizontal scroll
  • Multi-forge PR integration — the anyforge leaf
  • odu-runner — a runner you can reach with nothing running
  • odu-web — the service face of odu
  • pulam — the terminal-workspace surface as an ephemeral daemon on kaval
  • pulam-web — the browser twin (kolu's surface-consumption leg, proven standalone)
  • R2 — kaval, the standalone PTY daemon
  • Remote terminals over SSH
  • Sleeping terminals
  • Sundry
  • surface-connection — the WebSocket connection, owned upstream
  • Terminal Notes
  • The Code tab is a browser → @kolu/solid-browser
  • Welcome, revamped — pin it, reach it, run agents, watch it
  • Workflow viewer
